title: TrendLoom EA Tool
subtitle: Multi-Timeframe SMA Voting System — 7 Strategy Presets for Trend Direction
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: PARTIAL EXTRACT — the multi-TF voting/scoring pattern is reusable; SMA(50) as the sole indicator is too weak. The supermajority threshold concept (2-of-3 required, not 3-of-3) is a useful architecture variation.

core_concept.name: Multi-Timeframe SMA Voting
core_concept.description: Three timeframes each vote +1 (bullish), -1 (bearish), or 0 (neutral) based on whether close is above/below SMA(50). Sum the votes. >= 2 = BUY, <= -2 = SELL, else NEUTRAL. Supermajority (2-of-3) required.
core_concept.voting_formula: sum = sum(+1 if close[tf] > sma50[tf] else -1 if close[tf] < sma50[tf] else 0 for tf in [tf1, tf2, tf3]); result = 'BUY' if sum >= 2 else 'SELL' if sum <= -2 else 'NEUTRAL'
core_concept.supermajority_logic: Requires 2 of 3 timeframes to agree. This is softer than our current AND gate (3-of-3) but harder than OR (1-of-3). Allows for dissent on one timeframe — realistic in transition periods.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Our M15+M1 split is a 2-timeframe system. The voting architecture is reusable for multi-indicator scoring — sum gate values to produce a confidence score rather than hard AND rejection.

signal_logic.per_timeframe: signal[tf] = +1 if close[tf] > sma50[tf] else -1 if close[tf] < sma50[tf] else 0
signal_logic.aggregate: vote_sum = sum(signal[tf] for tf in selected_timeframes)

indicators_used.note: SMA(50) only — single indicator, three timeframes. No volume, volatility, structure, or oscillator.

steps.name: select_strategy
steps.description: User clicks one of 7 buttons to select timeframe combination

steps.name: fetch_bars
steps.description: Get last completed candle close for each of 3 selected TFs

steps.name: compute_sma
steps.description: Calculate SMA(50) on close prices for each TF

steps.name: vote
steps.description: Each TF casts +1/-1/0 vote based on price vs SMA

components_to_build.name: MultiTFVoter
components_to_build.contract: vote(bars_by_tf: dict, indicator_fn, threshold: int) -> (direction, score)
components_to_build.note: Generalize: accepts any indicator function, not just SMA. Pluggable.

backtesting.verdict: Author claims 'wonderful and profitable outcomes' — no statistics provided
backtesting.caveat: No win rate, profit factor, or drawdown data. Purely anecdotal.
backtesting.note: Author explicitly says tool is supplemental, not standalone signal provider.

keep: Voting architecture: +1/-1/0 per timeframe, sum to score — reusable for multi-indicator scoring
Supermajority threshold (2-of-3): softer than AND gate, realistic for transition periods
7 strategy presets as a concept — clear TF groupings for different trading styles
Score as output (not just binary) — score of +3 vs +2 are different confidence levels
discard: SMA(50) as sole indicator — too weak, no structure or volume context
GUI button panel — unnecessary for our Python pipeline
On-button-press execution — we run continuously, not on demand
improve: Replace SMA with our structural indicators: market_structure=BULLISH (+1), ADX rising (+1), EMA3 cross (+1) — same voting but better indicators
Add confidence score output: vote_sum as signal quality metric (3=high, 2=medium, 1=low, 0=skip)
Add weighted votes: H1 vote counts 2x M15 vote counts 1x — higher timeframes get more weight
Apply voting to gate chain: instead of binary PASS/FAIL, each gate contributes +1 to signal confidence score

creviaderiv_insights.gate_chain_as_voter: Our current entry gate chain is a strict AND: all gates must pass. Alternative architecture: each gate is a voter (+1 if pass, 0 if fail). Sum votes. Signal if sum >= threshold. This allows partial gate satisfaction and produces a confidence score.
creviaderiv_insights.confidence_score_idea: vote_sum = 0..5 (zone_touch + ema_cross + adx_rising + vwv_strong + departure_candle). Signal if >= 4. Report score in signal output. Higher score = larger position size.
creviaderiv_insights.supermajority_for_mtf: Our M15+M1 architecture: M15 structure=bullish (+2, weighted), M1 EMA cross=bullish (+1), M1 ADX=rising (+1). Require >= 3. This handles cases where M1 gives mixed signals while M15 structure is clear.
creviaderiv_insights.seven_preset_concept: Define our own strategy presets: 'Scalp' (M1+M5), 'Intraday' (M15+H1), 'Swing' (H1+H4+D1). Each preset has different gate thresholds and position sizing.
